stories.headline: Claude Computer Use lands on macOS: control your desktop from iPhone in research preview
stories.summary: Anthropic launched Claude Computer Use on macOS on March 24, allowing Pro and Max subscribers to message Claude from an iPhone and have it autonomously open apps, browse the web, fill spreadsheets, and interact with files on their connected Mac — no API configuration or code required.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to telegraphed locomotive control
stories.lateral_explanation: The earliest experiments with remote-controlled machinery in the 1890s placed an operator at a telegraph key miles from the machine. Claude Computer Use follows exactly this topology: the iPhone is the telegraph key, the Mac is the locomotive, and the agent is the protocol that translates intention into physical action. What changes is that the agent does not merely relay commands but interprets goals — the gap between telegraphy and agency is the gap between symbol transmission and meaning.

stories.headline: Claude Code Auto Mode + Cowork desktop control ship together: Anthropic's full-stack autonomous work bet
stories.summary: Anthropic shipped Claude Code Auto Mode — letting the coding agent execute tools without stopping for approval — and Cowork computer use for Claude.ai users, simultaneously on March 25. Together they represent Anthropic's full-stack bet on autonomous AI work.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to autopilot aviation certification
stories.lateral_explanation: Aviation autopilot systems went through a multi-decade certification process before operating without continuous pilot supervision — not because the technology wasn't capable but because governance frameworks had to be proven first. Claude Code Auto Mode and Cowork computer use are at the precise early autopilot moment: capable enough to operate unsupervised, but the governance layer is still being calibrated with each release.

stories.headline: GitAgent: open-source universal format decouples AI agent definition from execution framework
stories.summary: GitAgent stores an agent's definition as a structured Git directory, allowing a single agent to be exported to LangChain, AutoGen, CrewAI, Claude Code, or OpenAI Assistants without rewriting it. Includes built-in FINRA, SEC, and Federal Reserve compliance hooks.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to ISO shipping container standardization
stories.lateral_explanation: Before the ISO container, cargo was physically repacked at every port — a process accounting for 60-70% of total shipping costs. The container's innovation was not the box but the standard. GitAgent attempts the same intervention for AI agents: if the directory format becomes a standard, every platform can read and execute it, eliminating the repacking cost that currently makes agent portability impossible.

stories.headline: IBM turns 50 years of Masters Golf footage into a conversational archive with watsonx + Granite SLMs
stories.summary: IBM's Masters Vault Search makes 50 years of final-round Masters footage searchable through conversational prompts. Hole Insights calculates real-time scoring probabilities by cross-referencing live ball coordinates against historical data the moment a shot lands.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to oral history archive methodology
stories.lateral_explanation: Oral history archivists discovered in the 1970s that the most valuable thing you could do with recorded testimony was not preservation but activation — building finding aids that made the archive navigable. Masters Vault Search is the AI equivalent: fifty years of footage existed but was not findable through natural language. The agent layer converts a passive archive into an active epistemic tool — exactly what oral historians learned was the difference between preservation and use.

stories.headline: LangChain Deep Agents: structured runtime for planning, persistent memory, and context isolation in multi-step tasks
stories.summary: LangChain's Deep Agents framework gives AI agents explicit planning, per-subtask persistent memory, and context isolation for complex work taking hours rather than seconds. Scored ~42.65% on Terminal Bench 2.0 with Claude Sonnet 4.5, matching Claude Code at the same model tier.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to scientific experiment design
stories.lateral_explanation: Modern experimental design requires context isolation: each condition must be isolated from others to prevent contamination, which is why randomized controlled trials use blinding and separate treatment arms. LangChain Deep Agents imports this constraint into AI agent architecture. Agents that fail on multi-step tasks typically fail for the same reason badly designed experiments fail: information from one condition bleeds into another, and the results become uninterpretable.

stories.headline: NVIDIA and Emerald AI turn AI factories into dispatchable grid assets — demand response for data centers
stories.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI are integrating AI data center power consumption into grid management, treating AI factories as flexible loads that ramp up or down based on grid demand signals. A new economic model where AI operators generate revenue from grid balancing services alongside compute.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to aluminum smelter demand response economics
stories.lateral_explanation: Aluminum smelters have been used as grid-scale demand response assets since the 1970s — their massive, interruptible power consumption made them the original flexible industrial load. NVIDIA and Emerald AI are applying the same economic logic to AI data centers. The interesting implication: AI training jobs — unlike inference — can be shifted in time without affecting output quality, making them ideal demand response candidates.

stories.headline: GitHub: 4.3 million AI repositories — LLM-focused projects up 178% YoY as agent tooling replaces model training
stories.summary: GitHub's 2026 open source report reveals 4.3 million AI-related repositories, a 178% YoY increase in LLM-focused projects, with open-source activity shifting from model-training to agent tooling as the primary domain of developer differentiation.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Cambrian explosion in evolutionary biology
stories.lateral_explanation: The Cambrian explosion produced the majority of animal body plans in 20 million years — not a sudden acceleration but the moment environmental conditions allowed latent genetic variation to express as diversified body plans. GitHub's AI repository explosion follows the same logic: the underlying capability existed for years, but when the agent tooling layer became accessible, thousands of differentiated forms could suddenly express. The 4.3 million repositories are Cambrian organisms, and most will go extinct.

stories.headline: Workday delivers 1.7 billion AI actions in FY26 — Sana becomes its drag-and-drop Agent Development System
stories.summary: Workday reported 1.7 billion AI actions in FY26 and announced Sana as its Agent Development System — a drag-and-drop workflow builder letting business users construct custom AI agents without code. Transforms Workday into a platform where customers build their own AI workers.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to DEC PDP minicomputer democratization
stories.lateral_explanation: DEC's PDP minicomputers in the 1960s did something more significant than make computing cheaper: they put computing in the hands of domain experts without routing requests through centralized data processing departments. Workday's Sana is attempting the identical transition for AI agents: the drag-and-drop interface means an HR director can build an onboarding agent without routing the request through IT. The historical lesson from DEC: democratizing building tools produces more diverse applications than any centralized team could anticipate.

stories.headline: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite: 2.5x faster at $0.25/1M tokens — Google targets high-volume AI applications
stories.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite delivers 231.9 tokens/second — 2.5x faster and 45% faster output than Gemini 2.5 Flash — at $0.25/M input tokens, matching Gemini 2.5 Flash quality at a fraction of the cost. Google's direct response to Claude Haiku in the efficiency tier.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to generic drug market economics
stories.lateral_explanation: The generic drug market emerged when patents expired and competitors manufactured identical molecules at lower cost, collapsing original prices within 18 months. The AI inference market follows the same compression curve: within 18 months of a frontier model establishing a capability tier, a faster, cheaper version at equivalent quality appears. Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is the generic equivalent of Gemini 2.5 Flash. The strategic implication: the efficiency tier commoditizes rapidly, and differentiation only holds where quality is visibly superior.

stories.headline: obra/superpowers: shell-based agentic skills framework reaches 92,100 GitHub stars
stories.summary: obra/superpowers lets developers compose AI agent behavior through plain shell scripts rather than Python frameworks, reaching 92,100 stars and 7,300 forks on GitHub — one of the fastest-growing AI repositories in history. Shell-native approach eliminates dependencies on specific orchestration layers.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Unix philosophy
stories.lateral_explanation: Ken Thompson's Unix philosophy — write programs that do one thing well, write programs that work together — emerged as a reaction against monolithic software that tried to anticipate every use case. Shell scripting became the glue composing tools into complex workflows without coupling them. obra/superpowers applies the same philosophy to AI agents: rather than Python frameworks bundling routing, memory, and tooling into a single dependency stack, it uses shell scripts as the composition layer. The 92,100 stars are developers voting that the composition problem matters more than the framework problem.

stories.headline: eBay cuts 800 jobs — AI now handles 60–80% of inbound customer support tickets
stories.summary: eBay announced approximately 800 layoffs as AI and automated chat systems now handle 60–80% of inbound customer support tickets, directly displacing the roles that justified that headcount. One of the first major platforms to explicitly quantify the share of customer work AI has absorbed.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to telephone operator displacement
stories.lateral_explanation: Telephone operators numbered over 200,000 in the U.S. by 1940, performing functions considered too complex for automation. Automatic exchanges displaced most of the profession between 1950 and 1975, with remaining operators concentrating in complex and emotional interactions. eBay's 60-80% ticket automation exactly replicates the displacement curve: AI handles the routine and structured, while remaining headcount consolidates around the unusual and emotional. The historical lesson: the remaining 20-40% of work changes in character as well as volume — it becomes harder and stranger.

stories.headline: Pinterest eliminates 675 roles (~15% of workforce) — AI takes over content moderation and marketing
stories.summary: Pinterest confirmed approximately 675 layoffs as part of a pivot to an AI-forward strategy, with eliminated roles concentrated in content moderation and marketing. Notable: Pinterest named the technology directly rather than attributing cuts to restructuring.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to desktop publishing elimination of typesetting
stories.lateral_explanation: Desktop publishing in the 1980s eliminated commercial typesetting — not by making it easier but by moving it from specialists into the hands of whoever was creating the content. The skills did not disappear; they dispersed. Pinterest's AI strategy for content moderation follows the identical pattern: the work is now done by AI systems embedded in the creation workflow rather than a separate specialist review layer. The strategic consequence: the new gatekeeping capacity is cheaper and faster, but the editorial sensibility it embeds becomes harder to scrutinize.

stories.headline: Enterprise AI automation budgets drive 53% surge in AI Workers — corporations doubling AI spend to 1.7% of revenues
stories.summary: AI automation budgets are driving a 53% increase in use of autonomous AI Workers — agents handling complete job functions end to end — with corporations planning to increase AI spending from 0.8% to ~1.7% of revenues in 2026, the largest single-year jump in enterprise software budget allocation in history.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to electrification of factories
stories.lateral_explanation: Productivity gains from electrifying factories between 1890 and 1920 were largely invisible in aggregate output statistics for the first decade — because gains came not from adding motors to existing machinery but from completely redesigning factory layouts, requiring rebuilding rather than retrofitting. Enterprise AI is in the identical awkward period: the 53% budget increase is the investment phase before redesigned workflows generate the measured productivity payoff. The enterprises investing most aggressively in 2026 are likely to show the same delayed productivity spike that electrified factories showed in the 1920s.

stories.headline: Klarna's AI agent replaced 700 customer service employees — resolution time drops from 11 minutes to 2
stories.summary: Klarna's AI customer service agent automated two-thirds of all service chats — performing the equivalent work of 700 human employees — cutting average query resolution from 11 minutes to 2 minutes, a 5.5x efficiency improvement. One of the most precisely documented cases of AI workforce substitution in a public company.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Frederick Taylor's time-motion studies
stories.lateral_explanation: Taylor measured assembly work with a stopwatch to identify the theoretical minimum time per operation, then used that figure to calculate optimal staffing. Klarna's disclosure is a Taylor-style time-motion study applied to AI: the agent completes the same task in 2 minutes versus 11 for humans, immediately implying the staffing ratio required. The difference: Taylor's studies provoked labor organizing because workers could contest the measurement. Klarna's AI efficiency data is produced by the same system doing the work, making the measurement structurally uncontestable.

stories.headline: 9,238 of 45,363 tech layoffs in 2026 explicitly attributed to AI — corporate disclosure shifts
stories.summary: 9,238 of 45,363 confirmed tech layoffs — 20.4% — were explicitly attributed to AI and automation by companies in their public announcements, a sharp increase from prior years. Provides the first consistent dataset for measuring AI displacement pace in the tech sector.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Bureau of Labor Statistics displacement measurement
stories.lateral_explanation: The BLS spent decades trying to measure technology-driven job displacement, always running into the same problem: companies attribute layoffs to restructuring, never to the specific technology that changed the economics of labor. The result: economists could document aggregate trends but could not build causal chains from technology adoption to job loss. The 20.4% of 2026 tech layoffs explicitly attributed to AI represents a dataset historians of labor did not have for any previous technology transition.

stories.headline: Futurum: enterprises report 171% average ROI from agentic AI — U.S. companies averaging 192%
stories.summary: Futurum Research shows organizations deploying agentic AI report 171% average ROI — U.S. companies averaging 192% — driven by direct financial impact which nearly doubled as a primary metric from 11% to 21.7%. Enterprise AI is crossing from the investment phase to the return phase.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to ROI of factory electrification
stories.lateral_explanation: Analyses of factory electrification in the 1920s showed the typical factory earned back its capital cost within 2-3 years through energy efficiency, with secondary workflow redesign gains emerging later. The 171% agentic AI ROI maps onto a similar return profile: primary driver is operational cost reduction, with workflow redesign gains still accumulating. The divergence from electrification: factories took a decade to unlock secondary gains, but enterprises are reporting workflow restructuring gains within 18-24 months of deployment.

stories.headline: OpenAI to double workforce to 8,000 by end of 2026 — Agent Architects and Technical Ambassadors lead hiring
stories.summary: OpenAI plans to nearly double headcount from ~4,500 to 8,000 by end of 2026. Fastest-growing categories: Technical Ambassadors translating AI research into enterprise configurations, and Agent Architects designing multi-agent workflows. Announced simultaneously with products enabling headcount reductions at customer organizations.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Spinning Jenny paradox
stories.lateral_explanation: The Spinning Jenny eliminated hundreds of thousands of hand-spinning jobs in England between 1760 and 1800, while simultaneously creating a new industrial workforce of machine operators and factory managers. OpenAI simultaneously enables headcount reductions at customers and is hiring 3,500 new people itself. The Spinning Jenny paradox plays out in real time: the company building the displacement technology is building a new labor market around managing it. The net employment effect in textile districts was eventually positive, but the transition created two decades of visible displacement and invisible new hiring.

stories.headline: Salesforce FY26: record year as Agentforce drives 3x larger AI deals versus non-AI contracts
stories.summary: Salesforce FY26 was a record year driven by large-scale Agentforce deployments, with AI-driven deals averaging 3x the contract size of non-AI deals. Companies bought AI agent workflows handling lead qualification, case resolution, and customer onboarding autonomously. Benioff: Agentforce is the largest enterprise software category in history.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to CRM's own displacement of Rolodex sales culture
stories.lateral_explanation: CRM adoption in the 1990s-2000s displaced tacit sales knowledge from individuals into organizational infrastructure — the Rolodex culture where relationship knowledge lived in a salesperson's personal contact book was converted into institutional data. Salesforce has now automated both ends of the loop it originally created: first displacing the Rolodex with CRM data, now deploying AI agents that use that data to displace the human workflows the CRM was built to support. The strategic irony: Salesforce's most successful product makes the previous most successful product's human workflows redundant.

stories.headline: Agent Architects and Oversight Specialists emerge as 2026's defining new roles — 30–50% salary premium
stories.summary: Enterprise AI deployments are generating demand for Agent Architects — who design multi-agent systems and define boundaries of autonomous operation — and Oversight Specialists — who monitor AI worker performance and manage escalation protocols. CIO analysis shows these roles commanding 30-50% above equivalent software engineering compensation.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to nuclear safety officer profession
stories.lateral_explanation: The nuclear industry created Health Physics and Reactor Safety Officer professions not because physicists lacked technical knowledge but because the operational environment required dedicated individuals whose entire function was monitoring compliance and escalation — separate from those doing the physics work. Enterprise AI is creating the identical specialization: Oversight Specialists are the health physics officers of agentic systems. The salary premium reflects liability exposure — the person who signs off on an agent's operational boundaries bears a different risk profile than the engineer who built it.

stories.headline: IBM watsonx handles live Masters analytics for millions of viewers — no analyst in the loop
stories.summary: IBM's watsonx Orchestrate + Granite SLM system delivers real-time scoring probabilities and 50-year archive search for the 90th Masters Tournament across all platforms simultaneously — performing the work of sports statisticians, archivists, and broadcast researchers in real time, with no human in the analysis loop between ball landing and insight generation.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to sabermetrics and Moneyball
stories.lateral_explanation: Moneyball documented how baseball statistics had existed for over a century but the problem was not data availability but interpretation at speed. IBM's Masters system faces the identical structure: 50 years of footage and shot data existed, but interpreting it against live ball coordinates in real time required the watsonx agent layer. The Moneyball insight and IBM's deployment rest on the same foundation: speed of interpretation, not quality of data, is the competitive variable.

stories.headline: Teatralia cierra su 30ª edición el 29 de marzo: 30 espectáculos de 11 países en 17 municipios madrileños
stories.summary: La 30ª edición de Teatralia cierra el 29 de marzo con 29 compañías de 11 países y 30 espectáculos en 17 municipios de la Comunidad de Madrid, combinando teatro gestual, circo contemporáneo, danza, música en directo y teatro de objetos con programación accesible.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to medieval travelling theatre circuits
stories.lateral_explanation: Los actores itinerantes medievales no eran un estadio primitivo del teatro sedentario sino una forma artística distinta: su valor residía en llevar el teatro a donde la audiencia vivía, haciendo de cada aparición un acontecimiento extraordinario en un contexto ordinario. La distribución de Teatralia en diecisiete municipios sigue la misma lógica. La longevidad de treinta años confirma que el modelo nómada genera fidelización de audiencias que el modelo sedentario no alcanza en territorios periféricos.

stories.headline: Luces de Bohemia vuelve al Teatro Español con Eduardo Vasco tras barrer los Premios ADE
stories.summary: Eduardo Vasco repone Luces de Bohemia en el Teatro Español con Ginés García Millán y Antonio Molero, tras ganar el Premio ADE a la mejor dirección y el Premio Fotogramas de Plata para García Millán — la producción más premiada del teatro madrileño de los últimos dos años.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Situationist psychogeography
stories.lateral_explanation: Los situacionistas desarrollaron la psicogeografía en el París de los cincuenta para describir cómo el entorno urbano genera estados mentales distintos en quienes lo atraviesan. Valle-Inclán practicó la psicogeografía antes de que existiera el concepto: Luces de Bohemia mapea Madrid como sistema de estados de conciencia donde cada taberna y cada ministerio produce su variedad específica de humillación o grandeza. El Premio ADE confirma que el sector reconoce en el esperpento el marco más honesto para leer la ciudad.

stories.headline: Tristes sepulcros de nuestro amor llega hoy al ciclo Dramaturgia Emergente de los Teatros del Canal
stories.summary: Fernando Pérez Sañudo estrena esta noche Tristes sepulcros de nuestro amor en el ciclo Dramaturgia Emergente de los Teatros del Canal, el termómetro más fiable de la escritura teatral española en 2026. Formato de lectura de fragmentos + coloquio: la obra existe en borrador y la audiencia forma parte del proceso creativo.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to Shakespeare's workshop manuscript culture
stories.lateral_explanation: Los textos de Shakespeare no existían en versiones definitivas durante su vida: circulaban manuscritos alterados por actores y directores, y la versión impresa era frecuentemente una transcripción post-producción de lo que había funcionado en escena. La cultura del manuscrito de trabajo era el estado natural del texto teatral antes de que la imprenta impusiera la ficción del texto definitivo. El ciclo Dramaturgia Emergente restaura ese estado original: la obra que llegará a producción completa habrá sido moldeada por reacciones que el coloquio captura.

stories.headline: El Campero abre en Madrid su primer espacio fuera de Barbate: el atún rojo de almadraba llega a María de Molina
stories.summary: El Campero, el restaurante de Barbate que convirtió el atún rojo de almadraba en alta gastronomía, abre su primer espacio en Madrid en la calle María de Molina 25 — el primer restaurante del mundo dedicado en exclusiva al atún rojo de almadraba fuera de su lugar de origen.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to denomination of origin and terroir theory
stories.lateral_explanation: La teoría del terroir sostiene que el valor de un producto agrícola está parcialmente constituido por su lugar de producción — no sólo como factor de calidad sino como componente irreductible de identidad. La apertura de El Campero en Madrid plantea la misma pregunta que el mundo del vino cuando los vinos de terroir empezaron a distribuirse globalmente. La respuesta gastronómica que El Campero dará es que el método de captura es el terroir real del atún de almadraba, no la latitud del restaurante.

stories.headline: La hoguera de las maravillas llega el 27 de marzo al Corral de Comedias de Alcalá — Siglo de Oro en el teatro más antiguo
stories.summary: El Corral de Comedias de Alcalá de Henares acoge el 27 de marzo La hoguera de las maravillas, espectáculo basado en textos del Siglo de Oro, como cierre de la semana del Día Mundial del Teatro en Madrid. Textos clásicos en el espacio físico donde se representaron los contemporáneos de Cervantes.
stories.lateral_hook: connects to theatrical stratigraphy
stories.lateral_explanation: Los arqueólogos utilizan la estratigrafía para describir cómo los objetos de distintas épocas coexisten en capas en un mismo lugar físico. El Corral de Comedias de Alcalá funciona exactamente como un yacimiento estratigráfico: representar textos del Siglo de Oro en el mismo escenario donde fueron contemporáneos añade una dimensión de resonancia que ningún teatro moderno puede replicar. La experiencia no es museo sino activación: el edificio convierte el pasado en contemporáneo simplemente por existir.

books.title: Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI
books.author: Ethan Mollick
books.why: Mollick es el escritor más riguroso sobre cómo los profesionales reales cambian su forma de trabajar con IA. Tras una semana con datos de Klarna (700 empleados reemplazados, resolución en 2 minutos), OpenAI doblando plantilla hasta 8.000 y los nuevos roles de Agent Architect y Oversight Specialist emergiendo con un 30-50% de prima salarial, este libro da el marco conceptual para entender qué significa trabajar con IA a nivel individual frente a infraestructura empresarial.

books.title: AI Engineering: Building Applications with Foundation Models
books.author: Chip Huyen
books.why: Huyen escribió la guía definitiva para construir aplicaciones de IA en producción. Las historias de esta semana sobre GitAgent desacoplando la definición del agente del framework, LangChain Deep Agents con aislamiento de contexto por subtarea, y obra/superpowers alcanzando 92K estrellas desde una arquitectura shell-native apuntan al mismo tema: la ingeniería de IA se está bifurcando entre los que construyen sobre frameworks y los que diseñan los estándares de composición.
